IT WAS NOT REASONABLE FOR PETITIONERS TO INCUR FEES FOR THE WRIT FILED BY MR. HAYES. THE COURT CONFIRMS MS. MILES HOURLY RATE OF $200 WITH A MULTIPLIER OF 1.2% FOR THE 1ST AND 2ND MOTIONS. $200 PER HOUR IS A REASONABLE RATE FOR SOMEONE WITH MS. MILES' EXPERIENCE, THE LARGE AMOUNT OF TIME SHE SPENT ON THESE MOTIONS AND THE FACT THAT SHE PERFORMED FUNCTIONS THAT IN ANOTHER LAW OFFICE WOULD LIKELY HAVE BEEN PERFORMED BY A SECRETARY, CLERK AND/OR PARALEGAL. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) IN ADDITION, PETITIONER REQUESTED THE COURT FOR A STATEMENT OF DECISION. THE COURT ORDERED PETITIONER TO FILE HIS REQUEST AND EACH PARTY TO PROVIDE AUTHORITY AS TO WHETHER A STATEMENT OF DECISION IS REQUIRED. THE MATTER IS DEEMED SUBMITTED AT THE END OF THE DAY ON MARCH 9, 2012 AT WHICH TIME THE COURT, IF IT DETERMINES THAT A STATEMENT OF DECISION IS WARRANTED, SHALL ASK EACH PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER OF DECISION. LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER COALITION FOR ADEQUATE REVIEW, NINTY-NINE PERCENT AND ROB ANDERSON'S NOTICE AND MOTION TO STRIKE OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO TAX RESPONDENTS COST CLAIM. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE ENTIRETY OF THE CITY'S COSTS MEMORANDUM IS STRICKEN. THE PROCEEDINGS ON THE RETURN ARE NOT SEPARATE AND INDEPENDENT FROM THE PROCEEDINGS THAT LED UP TO THE RETURN AND THUS THE CITY IS NOT ENTITLED AS A MATTER OF RIGHT TO COSTS BECAUSE IT IS NOT A "PREVAILING PARTY" AS THAT PHRASE IS DEFINED IN CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1032(A)(4). ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT THE COURT HAS THE AUTHORITY TO AWARD COSTS IN ITS DISCRETION PER SECTION 1032(A)(4), THE COURT DECLINES TO DO SO BECAUSE PETITIONERS WERE THE PREVAILING PARTY ON THE INITIAL WRIT AND PETITIONERS, PETITIONERS ELECTED TO PREPARE THE RECORD ON THE RETURN, AND AN AWARD OF COSTS IS FUNDAMENTALLY UNFAIR GIVEN THAT PETITIONERS WERE NEVER PROVIDED WITH PAPER COPIES AND THE CITY DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT THE ELECTRONIC VERSION WAS HAD LITTLE OR NO UTILITY. MOREOVER, AN AWARD OF COSTS TO THE CITY UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES COULD BE VIEWED AS HAVING A DETERRENT EFFECT ON FUTURE CEQA PETITIONERS, WHICH IS CONTRARY TO THE ENFORCEMENT PROVISIONS OF CEQA, AND THE CITY'S HOURLY RATES FOR PARALEGAL TIME APPEAR TO BE SIGNIFICANTLY BEYOND THE ACTUAL COSTS FOR THOSE TIMEKEEPERS INCURRED BY THE CITY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

Petitioners' Notice Of Motion For Judgment Granting Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-23-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Petitioners' Motion for Judgment Granting Peremptory Writ of Mandate.

APPEARANCES: Mary Miles, Esq. 415/863-2310 for petitioners & moving parties Coalition for Adequate Review, Ninety-Nine Percent, Rob Anderson; Audrey Pearson, Deputy City Attorney 415/554-4621 for respondent & opposing party City and County of San Francisco.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Petitioners' Motion for Judgment Granting Peremptory Writ of Mandate is DENIED. The Supreme Court of California held that California Courts of Appeal, prior to ordering issuance of a peremptory writ in the first instance, provide notice that such a writ may issue, and invite informal opposition, in orders routinely called "Palma notices." (Brown, Winfield, & Canzoneri, Inc. v. Superior Court (2010) 47 Cal.4th 1233, 1238 (citing Palma v. U.S. Industrial Fasteners, Inc. (1984) 36 Cal.3d 171, 180)) A Palma notice is not required because this Court did not grant accelerated writ relief in the form of a peremptory writ in the first instance. The procedural history of this matter involved the usual procedures associated with the issuance of an alternative writ or an order to show cause. The Court denied Petitioners request for statement of decision. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kathryn Lloyd, CSR #5955 925/548-9114 (503/TLJ)

Ntc And Motion To Strike Or In The Alternative To Tax Respondents Coxt Claim; Memo Of P And A; Declaration Of Mary Miles In Support ; Declaration Of Rob Anderson
Off Calendar Jan-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER COALITION FOR ADEQUATE REVIEW, NINTY-NINE PERCENT AND ROB ANDERSON'S NOTICE AND MOTION TO STRIKE OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO TAX RESPONDENTS COST CLAIM.
Appearances: Mary Miles (863.2310) for petitioners and moving parties; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. The entirety of the City's costs memorandum is stricken. The proceedings on the return are not separate and independent from the proceedings that led up to the return and thus the City is not entitled as a matter of right to costs because it is not a "prevailing party" as that phrase is defined in Code of Civil Procedure section 1032(a)(4). Assuming without deciding that the court has the authority to award costs in its discretion per section 1032(a)(4), the court declines to do so because petitioners were the prevailing party on the initial writ and petitioners, petitioners elected to prepare the record on the return, and an award of costs is fundamentally unfair given that petitioners were never provided with paper copies and the City does not dispute that the electronic version was had little or no utility. Moreover, an award of costs to the City under these circumstances could be viewed as having a deterrent effect on future CEQA petitioners, which is contrary to the enforcement provisions of CEQA, and the City's hourly rates for paralegal time appear to be significantly beyond the actual costs for those timekeepers incurred by the City. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported
